data.title: Robinhood launches DOT
data.description: According to the official page, Robinhood has launched DOT.

data.title: Polygon Labs acquired two crypto startups, Coinme and Sequence, for a total of over $250 million.
data.description: Polygon Labs announced the completion of its acquisitions of cryptocurrency startups Coinme and Sequence for a total price exceeding $250 million. However, Polygon Labs declined to disclose the specific acquisition price for each company or whether the transactions were conducted through cash, equity, or a combination of both. Polygon Labs CEO Marc Boiron and Polygon Foundation founder Sandeep Nailwal stated that the acquisitions aim to support the blockchain network's stablecoin strategy. Coinme holds a series of remittance licenses in the United States, while Sequence focuses on building blockchain infrastructure, including crypto wallets. (Fortune)

data.title: Trump Media Technology Group Launches Four "America First" Themed Investment Strategies
data.description: Shares of Trump Media Technology Group (DJT.O) rose 1.7% in pre-market trading after the company launched four “America First” themed investment strategies. (Jinshi)

data.title: US inflation remained stable in December, with the overall CPI rising 2.7% year-on-year.
data.description: Data released by the U.S. Labor Department on Tuesday showed that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 2.7% year-on-year in December 2025, while the core CPI rose 2.6% year-on-year, both unchanged from November. Currently, Federal Reserve officials are divided on whether inflation or labor market risks should be prioritized, and they will closely watch Tuesday's report to assess how businesses are passing on tariff-related costs to consumers. This December CPI report is the first comprehensive review of inflation trends in several months. Due to the government shutdown last fall, the Labor Department was unable to collect price data on-site and had to use technical means to process missing data in the previous inflation report. Although the inflation rate is currently slower than it was several years ago, prices for necessities such as food and insurance remain significantly higher than before. The December CPI report indicates that inflation in 2025 is generally moderate, although there was a limited rebound in inflation during the summer. (Wall Street Journal)

data.title: U.S. December CPI and Core CPI Rates Remain Steady
data.description: According to PANews, the United States reported its December Consumer Price Index (CPI) year-on-year rate at 2.7%, matching both the forecast and the previous month's figure. The core CPI, which excludes food and energy prices, also remained stable at 2.6%, slightly below the anticipated 2.7% and consistent with the prior month's rate.

data.title: Teyana Taylor Celebrates Golden Globe Win with Satoshi Nakamoto Tribute
data.description: According to Foresight News, renowned singer Teyana Taylor marked her achievement of winning the 2026 Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress by wearing a jacket featuring the name of Bitcoin's anonymous founder, Satoshi Nakamoto.

data.title: The US December unadjusted CPI annual rate was 2.7%, below the expected 2.70%.
data.description: The US December unadjusted CPI year-on-year rate was 2.7%, below the expected 2.70% and the previous value of 2.70%. The US December unadjusted core CPI year-on-year rate was 2.6%, below the expected 2.70% and the previous value of 2.60%. (Jinshi)

data.title: US CPI annual rate met expectations, spot gold rose more than $10 in the short term.
data.description: The US December unadjusted CPI annual rate came in at 2.7%, unchanged from November and in line with market expectations. Following the release of the December CPI data, spot gold rose more than $10 in the short term, currently trading at $4613 per ounce; spot silver rose $0.30 in the short term, currently trading at $87.04 per ounce. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Interest Rate Projections for Early Next Year
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, ahead of the Consumer Price Index (CPI) release, CME's "FedWatch" tool indicates a 5% probability of a 25 basis point interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve in January next year, with a 95% chance of maintaining the current rate. By March, the likelihood of a cumulative 25 basis point cut rises to 24.3%, while the probability of rates remaining unchanged is 74.6%. There is a 1.1% chance of a cumulative 50 basis point cut by that time.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 910 USDT with a 1.10% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 13, 2026, 13:24 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 910 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 910 USDT, with a narrowed 1.10% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Teyana Taylor Sparks Bitcoin Interest with Satoshi Nakamoto Jacket at Golden Globe Awards
data.description: According to Odaily, renowned singer Teyana Taylor made headlines by wearing a jacket emblazoned with the name of Bitcoin's anonymous founder, Satoshi Nakamoto, after winning the Best Supporting Actress award at the 2026 Golden Globe Awards. This gesture is seen as a potential endorsement of Bitcoin's mainstream adoption.

data.title: Standard Chartered Bank: Ethereum's outlook has improved, and it is expected to outperform Bitcoin.
data.description: Standard Chartered Bank stated that Ethereum's outlook has improved, and it is poised to outperform Bitcoin. While Bitcoin's weak performance has dragged down the entire cryptocurrency market, growing institutional demand for Ethereum, along with its dominance in stablecoins, real-world assets, and decentralized finance (DeFi), supports its stronger prospects. Increased network throughput and potential clarity in US regulatory policies could further drive Ethereum's price up. Standard Chartered previously predicted that Ethereum's price would reach $7,500 this year and $30,000 by 2029.

data.title: ETHGas Announces Token Economics Model: Total Supply 10 Billion, Eligibility Snapshot to be Taken on January 19th
data.description: ETHGas published its token economic model on the X platform, revealing that its token GWEI has a total supply of 10 billion, with the ecosystem accounting for 31%, investors 27%, the team 22%, the community 10%, the foundation 8%, and advisors 2%. Furthermore, a snapshot of eligibility for the token airdrop will be taken on January 19th at 00:00 UTC.

data.title: Teyana Taylor wore a jacket with "Satoshi Nakamoto" printed on it after winning the Golden Globe Award.
data.description: After winning the 2026 Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress, renowned singer Teyana Taylor wore a jacket bearing the name of Satoshi Nakamoto, the anonymous founder of Bitcoin, seemingly in support of pushing Bitcoin into the mainstream. (BitcoinMagazine)

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Highlights U.S. Economic Resilience Amid Potential Risks
data.description: According to Odaily, JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has expressed confidence in the Federal Reserve's independence, noting that despite some underlying risks, the U.S. economy remains resilient. Dimon observed that while the labor market has slowed, overall conditions have not deteriorated. Consumers continue to spend, and businesses generally remain healthy. He suggested that this trend might persist for some time, supported by ongoing fiscal stimulus, deregulation benefits, and recent monetary policy from the Federal Reserve. However, Dimon maintains a cautious stance, emphasizing vigilance as the market seems to underestimate potential risks, including complex geopolitical situations, persistent inflation threats, and elevated asset prices.

data.title: Italy’s market watchdog warns crypto influencers as ESMA tightens rules on promotions
data.description:
The Commissione Nazionale per le Società e la Borsa (CONSOB) published a factsheet from the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) on Tuesday, reminding influencers that EU investment rules apply in the advertising of products like crypto. Italy’s securities commission has told social media “finance influencers” that promoting crypto tokens is not the same as […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/italy-warns-crypto-influencers-esma/

data.title: Spot silver broke through $86.244/ounce ahead of the CPI release, setting a new all-time high.
data.description: Ahead of the CPI release, spot silver broke through $86.244 per ounce, setting a new all-time high. (Golden Ten)

data.title: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon: Everyone I know believes in the independence of the Federal Reserve.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon stated that everyone he knows believes in the Federal Reserve's independence, and despite some potential risks, the U.S. economy remains resilient overall. Dimon noted, "While the labor market has slowed somewhat, the overall situation doesn't appear to have worsened. Meanwhile, consumers continue to spend, and businesses are generally healthy." He indicated that this trend could continue for some time, especially with ongoing fiscal stimulus, deregulation, and the support of the Fed's recent monetary policy. However, Dimon remained cautious: "We remain highly vigilant, and the market seems to be underestimating potential risks—including a complex geopolitical situation, persistent inflation risks, and high asset prices." (Jinshi)

data.title: Meta is shifting resources from the metaverse to AI devices.
data.description: Meta Platforms (META.O) announced over 1,000 job cuts at its RealityLabs division as part of a strategy to shift resources from virtual reality (VR) and metaverse products to artificial intelligence (AI) wearables and smartphone features. According to an internal memo from Chief Technology Officer Andrew Bosworth, employees will begin receiving layoff notices Tuesday morning. Previous reports from foreign media indicated that the layoffs will affect approximately 10% of Reality Labs' roughly 15,000 employees. A Meta spokesperson stated, "We mentioned last month that we would be shifting some investments from metaverse to wearables. This layoff is part of that strategy, and we plan to reinvest the saved resources to support the growth of our wearables business this year." (Jinshi)

data.title: Google Parent Alphabet Joins $4 Trillion Club on AI Momentum
data.description:
Alphabet Inc. has officially crossed a historic threshold, reaching a market capitalization above $4 trillion for the first time. The milestone was confirmed on January 13, 2026, placing Google’s parent company among the most valuable corporations ever and reinforcing its role as a central winner in the AI-driven market cycle
source: https://www.ethnews.com/google-parent-alphabet-joins-4-trillion-club-on-ai-momentum/
